evidence_index.text: Maria Jaramillo got a mammogram showing that a mass in her left breast had grown since her last exam.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Susan R. Ramos performed a wire localization, during which she inserted a wire into Maria's left breast and removed the mass.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At a follow-up appointment, an ultrasound revealed that a wire fragment remained in Maria's left breast.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Maria had the wire surgically removed but later died of unrelated causes.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Andrew B. Cramer, an expert witness, to testify at trial about the standard of care. She attached Dr. Cramer's declaration, in which he opined that the wire left in Maria's breast 'is something that can happen without negligence on the part of the surgeon.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Andrew B. Cramer

evidence_index.text: The district court granted it, finding that Dr. Ramos had rebutted the presumption of negligence by providing expert testimony about the standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: District court

evidence_index.text: We conclude that a genuine issue of material fact exists on the issue of negligence, we reverse the district court's grant of summary judgment and remand for further proceedings consistent with this opinion.
evidence_index.speaker: Supreme Court

evidence_index.text: We therefore clarify that NRS 41A.100(1), which relieves a plaintiff of the requirement to present expert testimony at trial, similarly relieves a plaintiff of this requirement at summary judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Supreme Court

evidence_index.text: Jaramillo alleged that Dr. Ramos unintentionally left a wire in Maria's left breast following surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The question thus becomes whether Jaramillo presented sufficient evidence that the facts giving rise to NRS 41A.100(1)s presumption existed. We conclude that she did.
evidence_index.speaker: Supreme Court

focused_exam.breast: Mass in left breast noted on imaging

critical_findings: Growth of mass in left breast

requestables.description: Mammogram showing growth of mass in left breast

reveal.result_summary: Mass in left breast has grown since last exam

requestables.name: Ultrasound

reveal.result_summary: Wire fragment remains in left breast
reveal.decision_impact: Required surgical removal of wire fragment

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with a breast mass

timeline_phases.description: Procedure to localize and remove breast mass

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Complications
timeline_phases.description: Follow-up appointment reveals retained wire fragment

decision_points.clinical_context: During wire localization procedure for breast mass removal
decision_points.prompt: What should be done to ensure complete removal of the wire?

options.description: Verify complete removal of wire with intraoperative imaging
options.clinical_reasoning: Ensures no foreign body is left in the patient

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded without verifying complete removal

expected_action_court.description: Verification of complete removal should have been done
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Standard practice to ensure no foreign body remains

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to verify complete removal led to retained wire fragment
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Intraoperative imaging should have been used to confirm removal
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Wire fragment surgically removed; patient later died of unrelated causes
patient_outcome.severity: No long-term harm from wire fragment
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: None related to the wire fragment

key_findings: Rebuttable presumption of negligence was not conclusively negated
Genuine issue of material fact exists on the issue of negligence

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to verify complete removal of wire
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Verification would have prevented retained wire

factual_timeline.event: Mammogram shows growth of mass in left breast

tests_performed.result: Mass in left breast has grown
Wire fragment remains in left breast

allegations.allegation: Failure to verify complete removal of wire

court_findings.finding: Genuine issue of material fact exists on the issue of negligence
court_findings.legal_significance: Reversed and remanded for further proceedings
